chunk_summary: Step-up elimination diets for EoE prioritize minimal restriction, starting with high-yield triggers like cow’s milk, wheat, and egg, followed by histology-guided reintroduction to confirm remission and prevent relapse.
learning_objectives: Understand the step-up elimination diet approach for eosinophilic esophagitis (EoE).
Learn the importance of histology-guided food reintroduction in EoE management.
Identify common food triggers in pediatric EoE and their prioritization in elimination diets.
Recognize the role of dietitians and the risks of restrictive diets in children with EoE.
key_facts: Step-up elimination diets often follow 2–4–6 food approaches, starting with fewer food restrictions to improve adherence and reduce endoscopy burden.
Cow’s milk is the most common trigger in pediatric EoE, followed by wheat, egg, and soy.
Histologic remission is defined as <15 eosinophils per high-power field (HPF) and must be confirmed before reintroducing foods.
Relapse is identified by histologic recurrence (≥15 eosinophils per HPF), not symptoms alone.
Dietitian involvement is critical to prevent malnutrition and address feeding dysfunction, especially in children with avoidant/restrictive food intake disorder (ARFID).
reference_quote: Step-up empiric elimination strategies (often described as 2–4–6 food approaches) remove fewer foods initially, improve adherence and quality of life, and can reduce the number of endoscopies compared with starting with six-food elimination.
document_key_topics: Step-up elimination diet strategies for EoE
Common food triggers in pediatric EoE
Nutritional and behavioral considerations in EoE management
